reviews.review_text:
[h1] PERFECTION EXPANDED [/h1]

[b][i]  Taking everything that made the original revolutionary and expanding it into a grand, cinematic masterpiece. Portal 2 elevates the puzzle design with brilliant new mechanics like propulsion and repulsion gels, while diving deep into the fascinating, decayed lore of Aperture Science.

The addition of Wheatley and Cave Johnson brings some of the funniest, sharpest dialogue ever written for a video game. Combined with an equally stellar co-op campaign that stands as one of the best cooperative experiences of all time, this entry is pure, unadulterated gaming perfection.

Goodbye, my only friend. 10/10  [/i][/b]
I love it the atmosphere and the story telling even on the credits

My bro and I were playing this game and got stuck on a level. We couldn't figure out what we were supposed to do, so we decided to take a break. About four years later, he and I finally came back and beat the game. I guess graduating from university, getting a job, and waiting for my frontal lobe to develop improved my puzzle-solving skills.

Huge storytelling, huge upgrade from Portal 1. This game is just brilliant.
Still replaying it years later and it’s still fantastic. 10/10, no notes.
Now we’re just waiting for Portal 3… but Valve seems allergic to the number 3 😂
— Sincerely, Wheatley

First of all, GLaDOS will be forever my hear me out.

If Portal was an apetizer, than Portal 2 is for sure a main course.
I do believe that it is nearly a perfect continuation. The only thing arguably wrong could be the fact that Portal had a constant feeling of isolation and horror, while Portal 2 doesn't feel that way - it has elements of it, obviously, but it's more of a storytelling comedy. I think it is because of the coop experience - how could you smoothly make a transition between horror main story and silly coop? You couldn't and that is why Portal 2 is more of a comedy.

First observation after starting the game is a simple "wow am I even playing Portal?".
It's much bigger game, has a lot more to tell, a lot more to experience. Snarky comments of our only proper AI - GLaDOS are just a chef's kiss. Wheatley presents less intelligent humour, however it's pretty entertaining. 
I guess the main question while creating Portal 2 was "how to immerse people better so they wouldn't feel it's a cheap copy?" and let me tell you - it is executed really well.
There is a ton of new mechanics, you get a proper storyline, you get fun coop experience and even community chambers which you can play if you already finished everything and you're starved for more.
Although I still wish that riddles would be harder. I felt it's a little bit too easy.

Achievements could be perfection if not for the Professor Portal one - I get it, we try to promote our game, find someone new to teach how to play Portal! It's a b*llshit of an achievement. As much as hugging three people is awesome - hugging your premade and some friends, teaching someone new to play is just an unfair marketing tactic, which now is difficult to be done, a LOT OF PEOPLE already played this game. 

Summarising, Portal 2 is how sequels should be done. Not using the reputation to earn money - GIVING an experience to your players, giving them what they want. This is probably why after this many years this game is still worth playing - it's made to be good. And it is good.
This game runs more smoothly than having a single empty tab open in your browser while overshadowing most modern AAA-games in storyline and ingenuity while competing in graphics.

In chapter 5, when they throw the turrets to the disposal area, you can only save 4 defective turrets and if you try to save more they explode :(

edit: so a bit later you can actually save all of the defective ones, so 10 / 10 game, would play again :D

Portal 2 is one of the amazing games valve had made . The storyline was improved from the previous one and the game features a lot new elements which improve the experience of the players and made it fantastic . The game finally came out of the testing chambers and give players to explore the dark world through the story .  Few chapters of this game is real challenging because the level design of this game is brilliant and it improved a lot from the previous game . This game really deserves better and we hope to see portal 3 in near future . Very underrated game series of the valve I have ever seen 

My personal rating to this game-9.8/10 :)

This is an amazing game. Literally felt like I was playing a video game and watching a movie AT THE SAME TIME. Yeah I got some spoilers before I played it. But that did not stop the game from being an absolute masterpiece. My favorite part was the part where [spoiler] you fall down to the old Aperture underground and have to portal around the mess. [/spoiler].

The puzzles in this game were also good. Loved them. Also merging the puzzles with the Portal 2 story didn't make me feel bored doing them. Also, the puzzle was satifying to solve so there's that.

The sound design is another reason why I like this game. Makes it feel immersive and cinematic.
I would listen to certain soundtracks sometimes because of how epic they are.

FINAL RATING 10/10

Absolute amazing title from Valve for a game I loved with a neat addition of humor and dark story.

And it was from back in 2011, you don't even realize it until you see the pre-rendered cutscenes.

Прекрасная игра, жаль утерян её прайм, но этот шедевр невозможно забыть настоящим ценителям игр, её стоит купить не только из-за очень крутого коопа, мастерской и тд, а из-за банального крутого сюжета, да как бывший обладатель пиратки могу вам посоветовать так сделать, но только ради сюжетки, но проходить платку очень клёво. Вообще по итогам, купите игру и не жалейте. Вальвам спс за то что создали такое чудо в 2011, жаль, что забытая легенда(((

[h3][b]A great sequel does not just repeat the formula, it makes it more entertaining[/b][/h3]

Portal 2 takes the foundation of the first game and expands it with much more personality. The story felt far more engaging and entertaining to me than in the previous entry, not only because of the humor and dialogue, but also because of how well it keeps the pace while introducing new mechanics that genuinely refresh the puzzles. I never felt like it was adding ideas just for the sake of it, and that makes progressing through Aperture more varied and more memorable. Its co-op campaign also works as a separate experience with its own story and characters, which gives it a lot of extra value.

I enjoyed it a lot because it manages to feel bigger without losing clarity. Solving the new chambers was always satisfying, and for once I felt the story pushed me forward more strongly than in the first game. My only warning is the co-op in 2026, because if I do not have friends who still own the game, finding someone to do the cooperative challenges with is not that easy. I strongly recommend it to anyone who enjoys puzzles, humor, and smart design, but less to those who only want action or plan to play everything alone.
An absolute classic, one of the best games ever made along with portal 1 :D
Recomendo este jogo, gosto da mecânica dos portais e de como resolver os puzzles.
the visuals were amazing, the voice acting was awesome, the challenges were actually challenging unlike the first game, wheatley is GOATED, and overall its just a great game. I would def recommend
ahead of its time. very fun and on of the best mechanics ive seen in a video game.

One of the best story games ever made. I first played this back on Xbox 360 and it ran beautifully. And as back then, it still runs the same today. Even if you have a potato pc, you will be able to play this game at playable frame rates. Nevertheless, great story and puzzle game. 100% recommended.

I completed Portal last week and decided to jump (no pun intended) into the second one directly after.
What was great in the first got improved. The game puzzle became tough from the second half of the game. Some can be very tricky as it's seeing a small gap.
The story is great, very funny.  A game from 2011 is putting to shame most of current game in 2026. Let that sink in.

I see that the COOP is a complete different story, if I find a buddy to do it, I will update this review about the coop.

It's all I hoped for, after over 10 years I finally got around to play it.

I'd certainly would get a Portal 3 if it were in the works :)

Portal 2 is a rare sequel that improves on its predecessor in nearly every way. Developed by Valve, the game combines brilliant puzzle design, exceptional writing, memorable characters, and polished gameplay into an experience that remains outstanding years after its release.

The core mechanic—using a portal gun to create linked portals and navigate increasingly complex test chambers—is easy to understand but allows for incredibly creative and challenging puzzles. Each new mechanic is introduced gradually, making the learning curve feel natural while constantly presenting fresh challenges. The puzzles are satisfying to solve because they reward logical thinking rather than trial and error.

The story is surprisingly engaging for a puzzle game. The interactions between GLaDOS and Wheatley provide some of the funniest and most memorable dialogue in gaming. The writing is clever, witty, and consistently entertaining, making even transitions between puzzle sections enjoyable.

Visually, the game still holds up remarkably well. The environments of Aperture Science Enrichment Center feel distinct and atmospheric, ranging from pristine testing chambers to fascinating abandoned sections that reveal the facility's history. The sound design and voice acting are equally impressive and contribute greatly to the game's immersive atmosphere.

Another major strength is the cooperative mode, which offers a completely separate campaign with unique puzzles designed specifically for two players. It encourages teamwork and communication while maintaining the same high standard of puzzle design found in the single-player experience.

Few games manage to balance challenge, humor, storytelling, and innovation as effectively as Portal 2. Whether you're a fan of puzzle games or simply looking for a memorable gaming experience, Portal 2 is an absolute must-play and remains one of the finest video games ever created.
If you have not played portal, you have not been on steam. even though the game is 10+ years old, there are many community ran mods and co-op mode. 9/10 could use more gameplay

This was my favorite game for about ten years, which I spent many more hours playing off a dvd on my childhood xbox. I then forced it out of my mind for about 5 years in effort to forget all I could to be able to experience it for the first time again. THAT is how good it is. Enjoy.
The perfect puzzle game. Took me a while to try it out but damn. The story and just overall puzzles are really good
A great sequel, one that still gets tons of community support to this day. The co-op mode is excellent and very worth bringing a friend along.
Good game love the story multiplayer is fun, Difficult puzzles overall great game
One of the best COOP gamemodes out there along with an almost perfect singleplayer campaign with challenging puzzles that feel extremely rewarding to complete.

One of the best games ever made sadly very breif but considering you get one of the best coop play throughs in it as well means you can get quite a bit more out of it. Even all these years later it not only holds up but I got a lot more out of it.

I had a crush on GLaDOS when i was younger so I recommend this game.
I love it the atmosphere and the story telling even on the credits
The dialogue and voice acting is fantastic.
Gameplay is fantastic.
Fantastic game.
Yep. Play it. Go on.
THERE IS SO MUCH CONTENT!!!!!!!!! I'll right something more detailed later, but to get this game, you don't need any, just get it (BUT PLAY PORTAL 1 FIRST)!!!!

One of the absolute best games ever made, Portal 1 was arleady Perfect but Portal 2 pushed the score even further. Everything from the graphics to the gameplay and characters everything is absolutely perfect. I highly recommend this game for everyone
You woudn't think a game with a silent protaganist would have on of the best written dialogue in videogame history
dünyanın gelmiş geçmiş en iyi puzzle oyunu oynamayan herkes hayat karalarını yeniden düşünsün, saygılar.

Another great puzzle game and a good sequel to Portal.
Characters are simple but well made with good humor.
All levels are creative and unique. Many new mechanics compared to first game.
If Portal showed me why I play games, then Portal 2 confirmed it. Absolute masterpiece!

bit late to the party but I get why its beloved.

Funny, challenging, and just generally entertaining, it feels like portal 1's vision fully realized and an absolutely beyond worthy successor.
I loved the first Portal game, and Portal 2 is even better! I love these puzzles so much.
Classic Co-Op brain game. Very fun. Requires communication & teamwork to complete puzzles.
one of the greatest games ever made. can be played many times and always fun to be had

This is a must have experience. Valve managed to out-do themselves with this title.
If you are someone who has experienced the Half-Life series and think highly of those games, you must also play this game. Why aren't you doing that?
Its a shame that, not enough people try the co-operative features of this game anymore. Last time I checked, this title had 1,980~ active players, according to SteamDB.
If you see Portal 2 on sale under $3, you make that purchase without ever giving it a second thought. You understand?

This is one of those rare games where almost everything just works. The puzzles are smart without feeling unfair, the writing is sharp, and the whole thing has that Valve confidence where it teaches you complicated ideas so smoothly you don’t notice you’re being trained like a lab rat with slightly better shoes.

The humor is a huge part of why it’s so memorable. GLaDOS is still perfect, Wheatley is a disaster in the best way, and Cave Johnson sounds like a man who could turn terrible business decisions into company policy with full confidence. The game is funny without stopping every five seconds to beg you to laugh, which is harder than it sounds.

I also love how the puzzles keep adding new layers. Portals, lasers, gels, light bridges, momentum, timing — it all builds naturally, and when you finally solve a room, you feel clever for about three seconds before realizing the game probably planned that feeling too.

And then there’s co-op, which is basically a friendship test with portals. Great idea, questionable communication, both players blaming physics. Perfect.

Still one of the best puzzle games ever made. Smart, funny, clean, creative, and somehow still fresh years later.

Very nice game, but i think portal 1 is arguably better, very smooth, almost always 200+ fps, on the highest graphics settings, i didn't play the coop tho, so it was for the singleplayer part, have a great day.

After playing every Valve game this really does feel like the culmination of everything Valve has learned about puzzles and platforming from all there single player games. The fact they added co op and the puzzles are designed around both players is insanely good level design. Just like half life 2 it feels like Valve designed source physics and a gun that interacts with everything first and the game followed. There are not a lot of games like portal and not a lot of items as iconic or fun as its main gun. The robot designs are cute and iconic. The characters are quite funny and memorable too. The ending is nice bow for the end of the series as well.
very good story and gameplay for a sorta old game definetly must buy

The first one was more innovative, but more of the same doesn't mean bad.
It means more of something good.
This game is just that. More of what made us love Portal. If you like puzzles, get it. If you don't, get it anyway. That's how good it is.
Games great played it twice and want to sock the british guy in the face. I would also bang the potato
I played with my friend randomly 2 times! Great RNG and a fun puzzle game.

woah, its almost like its some kind of portal, 2? hmmm i think i like that name




-john porr tall creator of the game
The one puzzle game that I can actually solve without looking up the answers or feel unchallenged! Genuinely a great game!
A classic of a game and still well worth playing today. The puzzles are great and the AI sphere is funny.

Extremely great story, amazing new additional mechanics compared to the first game, and a very excellent level builder for infinite replayability after completing both single and co-op campaigns. Overall, an amazing improvement off of the first game and a must-play puzzle game. I did find the final boss a little too easy and basic but I loved everything else.

i haven't beaten any other puzzle games besides TTP and portal, but i'd easily consider this the best puzzle game ever and an example of a great sequel. everything about this aged well in so many ways and the overall game was fun to the point where i had no complaints even in the end. i also like how you don't have to run this on a supercomputer despite the graphics being incredible and the game isn't too hard making this accessible for everyone. incredible game, without a doubt a 10/10
A very good addition to the first portal game better graphics, better gameplay. Recommended if you have already played the first one. Overall a brilliant game that you will loads of hours out of it

I've loved this game ever since I got it for christmas 2022 alongside my ps3. the humor is timeless, the puzzles are fun, and the robots are cute

overall i'd say this game is so good it made me forget i have 4 stds and a parasite

這款遊戲並非只是動畫效果更佳、卡牌更新的《殺戮尖塔》。初代《殺戮尖塔》的核心精髓依然保留，但遊戲本身已煥然一新。新職業趣味十足，我特別喜歡對老職業的改動，例如缺陷者職業現在有更多臨時專注選項，而非永久專注。

我一直在測試服體驗，開發團隊確實會聽取玩家回饋，並積極更新遊戲以提升體驗（例如削弱無限連擊）。我非常期待1.0正式版發布後，這款遊戲會呈現出怎樣的面貌，包括新增的劇情模式、真實結局等等。

Portal 2 is very fun and expands on Portal 1’s gameplay, but it loses the horror aspect of the original and has way too many “le funny Reddit” moments.
It’s better than Half-Life 2, but not as good as Portal 1. Play this if you enjoyed the first game and want more puzzles and gameplay.
8/10

Has some proto woke elements  to it but  it's kino
co-op is SO fun if other friends have portal 2. and also the main solo game is very fun too, lots of tricky puzzles to keep you going :]

comming from portal 1 this game amped up everything good about the pervious game. And did things beyond that.  Thoroughly enjoyed the playthrough. story and humor throughout is even better this time around.  graphics, visuals and the artstyle is so freaking good. 9/10 easily for me.

(Slight spoiler alert.) I think this game is one of the best games I've ever played. One of my favourite things about it is that the storyline is exceptional. While GLaDOS DOES, admittedly, insult you throughout nearly the whole game up until she becomes a potato, it's just silly jokes and shouldn't be taken personally. The way Wheatley was designed visually is incredibly detailed, and his attitude and the way the game portrays his moronic features overall was outstanding to me. It shows a lot of new features from Portal 1, and is a fun sequel to learn there's more to Aperture than you first thought in Portal 1. While it has an entirely new design and so much more attention to detail, somehow Portal 2 keeps that original Portal vibe. The Co-op mode is well-built, too. At the end of the game, when you shoot the moon, it was an incredible idea. Seeing as you learn moon-rock conducts portals excellently in chapter 6, I have to give it credit as eventually Wheatley hints that the moon can be shot. His words are "Keep looking at your precious human moon. It won't help you now!" But of course obviously it will help you, as it is the only thing you can do. Apology if the review was too long.

I remember playing this game when I was younger playing it on the Xbox 
This game is really great at story telling and the puzzles are just perfect 
Although I spent 20mins on a few levels

I also love trolling my friends in multiplayer

Great game that doubles the power of its predecessor,

Portal 2 is responsible for a super well paced story (with phenomenal villains) that centers around very clear, but always engaging, puzzle solving. This game maintains stakes, energy, and pace while revolving around only puzzle solving. The co-op campaign is a thing of beauty; great OG co-op that has super fun puzzle-solving design.

Great solo or team adventure. Grab a pal. Eat cake or whatever

Great puzzle & survival horror. Not as scary as the first game, but still pretty scary. Can be hard at times, but the puzzles don't matter. The game and lore and story are extremely good. 8/10. Also runs amazingly on low-end PCs. No mac support, though.

One of the best puzzle games of all time, if you have been sleeping on Portal 2 I recommend you take the plunge. The dialog is fun and the voice acting amazing, and the puzzles, while difficult, prove to be solvable if you take a little time with them.
A must-play for anyone who enjoys puzzles. I've had so much fun playing it!
Portals and physics and a cool story campaign and player mad map support? Awesome! Also community made mode like Portal: Reloaded or Community Edition.

Portal 2 is the game that is part of the portal series. It was relesed after portal and before portal 3. luckily for portal 2 however, it has always wanted to be the youngest child to recieve the majority of the familys love. And therefore sucessfully prevented the creation of portal 3 via condom. This does sadly imply that my second sentance is incorrect due to portal 3 not being something that exists in out reality. 

in the game portal 2 you play as either a ball robot or a tall robot, one of which not being acessable for you since you dont have the connections neccisary to play multiplayer. Luckily for you there is a single player option, meaning that you can experience the scary entrance scene dude aswell as the rest of the game alone and sad. And as the cherry pile being dumped on the cake via truck, there is a constant ai voice roasting you and only you through out the game. Incase you feel like you matter, this will multiply you by light twice.

The core mechanic of both portal games (including the first one), is suprisingly portals, which is something that even i couldn't guess. These are created using your company issued portal gun. The colors being shot out are (0,0,255) and the color \sqrt{6\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{n^{2}}} usually has. These portals can sadly only be used on certan surfices, which are the white ones in singular since they are the only ones that can be portaled to. sometimes you will find water on the ground, which is terrible compound design by the ones who designed the building you play in. Unfortunately for you, this water is not flowing. Meaning that you somehow aren't fine after touching it, since robots somehow now can die of brain eating amoeba.

One of my favorite mechanics in the game is something that you'll never experience since it requires who people. Therefore i dont even recomend you reading this paragraph. but the mechanic that im talking about are the shared emotes that you can do. As an example you can force your companion to do a high five with you, or if you're playing as the long guy in multiplayer (which you aren't), you can take your "friends" ball shaped torso from him / her. But be careful, your friend might come to your house and bash your head with a frying pan if you do that.

In conclution, portal ೨ is a game where you survive and dont die via non robotical deaths, which is impossible since robots cant die. 

1 thousand / 1000  Portal 2 - The Final Hours

A masterfully constructed experience, and a must play.
A close friend really loved this game, so I thought I'd give it a shot...
Long story short, I couldn't put it down until it was finished, and then I immediately bought and played the original that same day.
This is honestly such a great time, with an awesome look, great humour, and very fun puzzles.
Now stop reading this review and go buy this masterpiece.
Top 2 games oat. Its eerie feeling at times and having a dystopian setting compliments the game very well. Also doesnt need a good pc to run
game is amazing and runs very well on a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ pc, w optimization.
Coming back to this game after 14 years, and it is still one of the best games of all time.
Just as peak as every other valve story game. Play the first one, first! Took me about 6 hours for this one and 3 for the other one. good stuff.

The best puzzle game ever released, it is funny, it is immersive, it will find you scratching your head thinking inside and outside the box to get to the end of each level. Incredibly short but one of the sweetest games you will ever play
A very original, funny and witty game combining logic puzzles and action!
it followed really well after the first part the story was greater and the graphics are still really good after this game being this old!

i thought this game was more fun/polished than portal 1 but also it doesn't really introduce the story from portal 1 very well. it's more like a continuation and i get the sense that they were intending people to have played the first one first and then this one. but i found this more fun than portal 1. i wouldn't start with this game though

I had 28 hours in this game and never played the singleplayer mode haha.

Now I have, it's amazing. Can definitely recommend this game.

Turn on "Raw Mouse Input" in settings if you want looking around not to feel like ♥♥♥♥.

It runs great for my bad computer despite it having some heavy graphics. The story is very creative & unique, making the game an awesome sequel to the first game. They use the new mechanics in interesting ways, the gels are very fun to use but hard to master. Wheatley and GLaDOS are some of the best characters in the game, with great humor and voice acting. Even after the main game, Valve added community maps, so you can always have something to do after beating it.

9.5/10.
A masterpiece of a puzzle game and i can never get over how beautiful the ending song is

What if you took a game, a game that was already really good, and made a sequel of said game by making it like 10 times better? Whatever example you may have in your head, throw it out the window and revel in the beauty of the sequel to the first Portal, Portal 2. If the first Portal is a unique experience, then Portal 2 is a drug designed to stimulate you out the wazoo and boy howdy does it deliver. The callback to the first game, the references to the world around you (y'know, the other Valve titles), and the sheer amount of lore and insults thrown your way is commendable. In the first Portal, it's a game that was clearly designed in an earlier era of gaming, by an up and coming studio that was testing some real cool stuff. Portal 2 is the result of letting developers work their magic and understand more about what it is the players want in a game, and more importantly, a Portal game. Portal 2 delivers far more content than the first title, be it by simply giving you more puzzles to work with, or by going around the Aperture Science facility (instead of being trapped in the fairly sterile rooms like in the first game). Not to mention, a ton more voice acting and interaction with the AI that make you feel less like a test subject and more like an escapee. It may not be a long game, but there's also the multiplayer action that can supplement a ton more hours with friends, and quite a bit of support within the modding community. All in all, it's a lovely game that I do believe everyone with the hardware should try.

And, as always, I will be waiting for the day Gabe can count to the legendary number "3".
This game is amazing is manged to become my most favourite game of all time. Infinite replayablity considering ive replayed this dozens of times. Highly suggest buying this game

Really good if you're bored and looking for something to do. It can get really tricky at times, but when you look back at it, the solution often seems obvious. Overall, a fun and satisfying game.

-8/10

Best value for money game ever, Both single player and co-op modes were very well made and worth the price only downside i have faced might be that my last remaining brain cell has essentially resigned from too much workload.

Was it 10/1 or 10 x 10???? Oh well HIGHLY RECOMMENDED

It's Portal 2 yes I recommend this game lol

Refreshing to go back to this game every once in a while, it has aged like wine. Still clever, still funny, still well designed, characters are still great. Really wish Valve would make games more often!
Portal 1 is the second best puzzle game, this is the first.

A puzzle game worthy of the name! Much longer than its predecessor, but that is hardly a bad thing given how genuinely entertaining and well-scripted everything is. On the flipside however, it is Portal. Your patience will be tested. Sanity, too.
Crazy story game including coop. I just LOVE this game. I strongly recommend you playing it because it is so overlooked by the players. 10/10
This game is legend, Valve, big clap! So cinematic, humorous, but the best is the old factory's feeling, it makes the game truly unforgettable!
the game has a good story and a bunch of user made content so you wont have to buy a new game, its co-op game mode is really fun even with random people. the community is very kind
game kept getting better and better and had 10/10 ending
Amazing game. No comments, absolute masterpiece. This game made me love videogames again, and this might be one of the few games I will actually replay in my life time.
Good puzzle game, and the multiplayer campaign is also quite fun with friends. The puzzles truly have you thinking with portals.
Great sci-fi story, and characters. Especially my favorite moron core

Amazing game, an improvement on its predecessor if that was possible, with an awesome co-op campaign that has you and a friend really thinking with portals!
Amazing experience I Highly Recommend it for anyone who wants something new and fresh

I bought portal 1 and portal 2 on the same day i played portal 1 in one day it was alot calmer than this seventh layer of hell i actually havent been this mad at a game since i played CSGO for the first time i couldve played through portal 2 in one day but no i had to take actual breaks irl because this game made me so mad either way its a great game and great franchise i would recomend

I got this game 10 years ago to play with my friend because it was the only game I could run on my computer. That computer crashed and I finally finished the story mode. I had a great time testing with Glados again!
Must be one the most quintessential puzzle games out there for any type of gamer...yeah thats it...thats the review.
